CVE-2026-63030.title: wp2shell — REST API batch-route confusion → RCE
CVE-2026-63030.role: Entry point of the wp2shell chain. A route-confusion flaw in the REST batch endpoint lets an unauthenticated request reach an internal query path; chained with CVE-2026-60137 it yields unauthenticated remote code execution.

CVE-2026-60137.title: author__not_in WP_Query SQL injection
CVE-2026-60137.role: Second link of the wp2shell chain. Unsanitised input in author__not_in reaches the database query. Present since 6.8 — the 6.8 branch is exposed to this SQL injection on its own, but not to the full RCE chain (which also needs CVE-2026-63030).

CVE-2026-3906.title: Notes REST API — missing authorization (arbitrary note creation)
CVE-2026-3906.role: The Notes feature (added in 6.9) skipped the edit_post permission check in its REST endpoint, letting a Subscriber create notes on any post — including private and other users' posts. Only fully fixed in 6.9.4 (partial fixes in 6.9.2/6.9.3).

evidence.detail: batch/v1 namespace is registered (REST batch endpoint reachable)

notes: Detected version is exposed to the full wp2shell pre-auth RCE chain (CVE-2026-63030 + CVE-2026-60137). Treat any internet-facing instance that ran this version as potentially compromised — patching closes the route but does not remove a backdoor planted beforehand.
